data.hero_eyebrow: Pool, spa, pond & fountain equipment — Dubai & the GCC
data.hero_sub: Aquora supplies the pumps, filtration, heating and automation that keep Gulf pools, spas and water features running flawlessly. Specified by contractors, trusted by villa owners and hospitality groups across the UAE.

sections.heading: Curated equipment, no compromise
sections.body: Every line we carry is chosen for the realities of Gulf water and climate — high mineral loads, intense heat and year-round operation. From the Aquora Pro pump and filtration range to the Lumina LED, Thermaline heating and Pure dosing systems, you get hardware that performs season after season. No grey-market guesswork, just equipment specified to do its job.

sections.heading: Design & installation, done properly
sections.body: Tell us the project and our engineers size it correctly the first time — flow rates, turnover, filtration and energy load all accounted for. We supply full equipment schedules for new builds and renovations, and work alongside your contractor or ours. The result is a plant room that runs quietly, efficiently and to specification.

sections.heading: In stock, on site, on time
sections.body: We hold deep inventory in the UAE so your project never waits on a shipment. Most orders ship within 48 hours across Dubai, Abu Dhabi and the Northern Emirates, with delivery to the wider GCC on request. Replacement parts and consumables are kept stocked, so a breakdown is a phone call, not a setback.

sections.heading: Expert support & real warranty
sections.body: Our team has commissioned and serviced Gulf water systems for over fifteen years, so the advice you get is practical and proven. Every product carries a clear manufacturer warranty, backed locally by Aquora. When you need spares, sizing help or troubleshooting, you reach a specialist who knows the equipment — not a call centre.

trust_signals: Built for Gulf water & heat conditions
Specified by leading contractors & hospitality groups

items.q: Do you deliver across the UAE and the wider GCC?
items.a: Yes. We deliver to every emirate across the UAE as standard, and ship regularly to Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, Bahrain and Kuwait. Smaller items move via tracked courier, while pumps, filters and bulk consumables are dispatched on our own logistics or trusted freight partners. Delivery to mainland UAE addresses is typically within 1-3 working days once an order is confirmed.

items.q: What are your typical lead times and do you hold stock locally?
items.a: Core equipment and consumables from our Aquora house lines are held in our UAE warehouse for same-week dispatch. In-stock items usually ship within 1-2 working days, while made-to-order or larger commercial systems run on a quoted lead time, generally 2-4 weeks. We confirm exact availability at the point of order so you can plan your project with confidence.

items.q: Do you offer installation and commissioning?
items.a: We do. Our certified technicians handle full installation, hydraulic balancing and commissioning for pool, spa, pond and fountain systems across the UAE. Every commissioning includes a performance check, water-balance verification and a handover walkthrough so your system runs correctly from day one. Installation is quoted per project, and we also support your own contractor with technical guidance where preferred.

items.q: What warranty cover do you provide, and are spare parts available?
items.a: All Aquora equipment carries a manufacturer-backed warranty, typically 12 to 24 months depending on the product line, with extended cover available on selected commercial systems. We stock genuine spare parts and wear components locally, so seals, cartridges, impellers and consumables are readily available without long import waits. Keeping spares in-country means faster repairs and minimal downtime for your installation.

items.q: What payment options do you accept?
items.a: We accept bank transfer, all major credit and debit cards, and cash on collection for walk-in orders. Approved contractors and trade accounts can apply for credit terms, typically 30 days, subject to a short approval process. For larger commercial supply, we can structure staged payments tied to delivery and commissioning milestones.

items.q: Are your prices inclusive of VAT?
items.a: Prices are shown exclusive of VAT unless stated otherwise, with 5% UAE VAT applied at checkout and itemised clearly on your invoice. All invoices are fully tax-compliant and include our TRN for your records. GCC export orders are handled under the applicable cross-border rules, which we confirm on the quotation.

items.q: Do you offer bulk or contractor pricing?
items.a: Yes. We run dedicated trade pricing for contractors, consultants and facilities buyers, with tiered discounts that scale to order volume. Registered trade accounts also get priority stock allocation, project-based quotations and a named account contact. Get in touch with your project scope or anticipated annual volume and we will build a tailored rate for you.

Unused items in their original, sealed packaging may be returned within 14 days of delivery for a refund or exchange. Made-to-order, custom-configured and special-import products are non-returnable, and a restocking fee may apply to certain bulk orders. If anything arrives faulty or incorrect, we will replace it promptly at no cost to you, just contact us with your order reference.

items.q: What technical support do you offer after purchase?
items.a: Our technical team is available to help with sizing, troubleshooting, water chemistry and system optimisation, by phone, email or on-site where needed. We provide guidance for both end users and contractors, from selecting the right pump to diagnosing a performance issue. For commercial clients, we also offer scheduled maintenance and service agreements to keep systems running reliably.

items.q: Do you ship outside the UAE?
items.a: Yes. Alongside full UAE coverage, we export across the GCC and to selected wider markets on request. Cross-border orders are quoted with delivery timelines, freight and any applicable duties confirmed upfront, so there are no surprises. Share your destination and requirements and we will advise the most efficient shipping route and lead time.

data:
# About Aquora

## Engineered in Dubai, built for the Gulf

Aquora began with a simple frustration shared by engineers and villa owners alike: too much of the equipment sold into the region's pools and water features was specified on price rather than performance, then left without proper support once the installer had driven away. We started in Dubai to do the opposite — to bring genuinely engineered water systems to the GCC, backed by people who stand behind them.

From a single workshop and showroom, Aquora has grown into a trusted supplier of pool, spa, pond and fountain equipment for residential and commercial projects across the Emirates. Our approach has never changed. We treat every pool as a piece of engineering — a balance of hydraulics, filtration, chemistry and energy — and we specify accordingly. That discipline is what separates water that simply looks clean from water that is genuinely safe, stable and efficient to run in the Gulf climate.

## A complete catalogue, engineered to work together

Our house product lines cover the full water cycle. We supply variable-speed circulation pumps, high-flow sand and cartridge filtration, energy-efficient heat pumps and heating, automated dosing and sanitisation, and precise LED lighting and control systems. Beyond the swimming pool, our range extends to spa and hydrotherapy equipment, pond life-support and filtration, and architectural fountain systems — nozzles, pumps and controls for everything from a courtyard feature to a hotel landmark.

Because we engineer these ranges to operate as one system, components are matched for flow, pressure and chemistry rather than bolted together from whatever was in stock. The result is quieter plant rooms, lower energy bills and water that holds its quality through a Gulf summer.

## Design, build and genuine after-sales support

Alongside supply, our design-and-build arm works with consultants, contractors and private clients from concept to commissioning. We size and lay out plant rooms, model circulation and filtration, integrate automation, and hand over systems that are documented and serviceable — not a mystery to whoever maintains them next.

That commitment continues long after handover. Aquora supplies only authentic equipment, sourced through proper channels, so spares, warranties and performance are exactly what they should be. Our team provides ongoing servicing, parts and technical guidance across the UAE and the wider GCC, supporting clients in Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, Bahrain and Kuwait.

We build water systems to last — and we stay to look after them.

**Talk to Aquora about your project.** Whether you are specifying a single villa pool or a hospitality landmark, our engineers are ready to help.

The Services page is written and saved.

The page runs ~460 words and is structured around the full project lifecycle with the requested subheadings:

- **Consultation & Hydraulic Design** — site survey, turnover/pipe sizing/pump head calculations
- **Equipment Specification** — Aquora house product lines, sized for Gulf water chemistry and heat
- **Supply** — warranty-backed plant, AED pricing, villa owners and contractors
- **Installation & Commissioning** — certified teams, pressure-testing, documented commissioning
- **Fountains** — light and musical/"dancing" fountains, choreographed water/colour/sound
- **Maintenance Contracts** — scheduled care, single villa to multi-site portfolios
- **Request a Free Consultation** — closing CTA

All copy is original, avoids real manufacturer trademarks (uses "Aquora house product lines" and generic engineering language), uses AED, and holds the confident/expert/premium-but-practical UAE-English voice.

Assistant# How to Choose the Right Pool Filtration System for a UAE Villa

Your filtration system is the single most important piece of equipment on your property. Get it right and the water stays clear, balanced and inviting with minimal fuss. Get it wrong and you'll battle cloudiness, blocked equipment and rising chemical costs all summer. In the UAE, where fine dust and extreme heat work against you year-round, choosing well matters more than almost anywhere else. Here's how to make the right call.

## The Three Media Types

**Sand filtration** is the workhorse of the villa pool. Water passes through a bed of graded sand, trapping particles down to roughly 20–40 microns. It's robust, low-maintenance and inexpensive to run. The trade-off is fineness: very small dust particles can pass straight through. The Aquora **Vantage Sand** series suits most standard residential pools and is forgiving of the occasional missed service.

**Cartridge filtration** uses a pleated element with a far larger surface area, capturing particles down to 10–20 microns. The water clarity is noticeably better and there's no backwashing, which conserves water — a genuine advantage given UAE tariffs. The catch is that cartridges need periodic removal and rinsing, and replacement every couple of seasons. Our **ClearLine Cartridge** range is ideal for owners who prioritise water quality and want to minimise waste.

**Glass media** is the modern upgrade to sand. Recycled, precision-graded glass filters finer (down to around 5–10 microns), resists biofilm thanks to its smooth surface, and lasts considerably longer than sand before it needs replacing. It also requires less backwash water. For UAE conditions, **Aquora ActiveGlass** is often the best-value long-term choice — it slots straight into most existing sand tanks.

## Sizing by Volume and Turnover

The golden rule is **turnover**: your system should circulate the entire pool volume in roughly 4–6 hours, giving 4–6 turnovers per day. In the Gulf's heat and dust load, aim for the faster end — four hours.

Start with your pool volume in litres (length × width × average depth in metres × 1,000). A typical 8m × 4m villa pool at 1.5m average depth holds about 48,000 litres. To turn that over in four hours, you need a flow rate of roughly **12,000 litres per hour**, and a filter and pump rated to match. Always size the **filter** to the flow rate, not just the pump — an undersized tank chokes performance and shortens media life. When in doubt, size up: an oversized filter runs more efficiently and lasts longer.

## The Gulf Factor: Dust and Heat

This is where UAE pools differ from anywhere else. Fine shamal dust settles continuously, loading your filter far faster than in temperate climates. Sustained water temperatures above 30°C accelerate both algae growth and chemical demand, meaning your filtration has to work harder to keep ahead.

Two practical implications. First, lean towards **finer media** — glass or cartridge — to capture the airborne dust that sand lets through. Second, **run longer cycles** in summer: 8–10 hours of daily filtration is realistic from May to September. Pairing your filter with a variable-speed pump keeps running costs sensible while maintaining turnover.

## Staying on Top of Maintenance

- **Sand/glass:** backwash when the pressure gauge rises 0.3–0.5 bar above its clean baseline. Replace sand every 3–5 years; glass every 7–10.
- **Cartridge:** rinse every 2–4 weeks in summer, deep-clean monthly, replace every 2–3 seasons.
- **All systems:** check the pressure gauge weekly and keep the pump basket clear of debris.

## The Bottom Line

For most UAE villas, glass media offers the best balance of clarity, water efficiency and longevity against our dust and heat. Cartridge suits quality-focused owners; sand remains a dependable budget option. Size for a four-hour turnover, run longer in summer, and service on schedule — do that, and your pool stays effortlessly clear.

*Speak to the Aquora team for a free filtration assessment tailored to your pool and location.*

Here is the article.

```markdown
# Saltwater vs Chlorine Pools in the Gulf Climate

Few decisions shape a pool's day-to-day character more than how it is sanitised. In the Gulf, where pools run almost year-round and ambient temperatures regularly push past 40°C, the saltwater-versus-chlorine question carries real weight for comfort, cost and longevity. Both systems keep water safe and clear. The difference lies in how they get there, and what that means for you over a decade of ownership.

## How Each System Actually Works

It is a common misconception that a "saltwater pool" is chlorine-free. In reality, a salt pool *generates* its own chlorine. A salt chlorinator passes pool water across an electrolytic cell, splitting dissolved salt into chlorine on demand, which then reverts to salt after it has done its work. A conventional pool, by contrast, relies on chlorine dosed manually or via a feeder — as tablets, granules or liquid.

So the real comparison is not salt against chlorine, but *generated* chlorine against *added* chlorine.

## Comfort and Water Feel

This is where saltwater earns its reputation. At the low salinity used in pools — roughly a third as salty as seawater — the water feels noticeably softer on skin and gentler on eyes. There is no sharp chemical tang, and swimmers prone to irritation often tolerate salt pools better. In a climate where families swim daily, that everyday comfort matters.

Traditional chlorine pools are perfectly swimmable, but fluctuating manual dosing can produce the occasional harsh batch or the familiar "chlorine smell" — which, ironically, signals chloramines rather than clean water.

## Running Cost Over Time

Upfront, a chlorine pool is cheaper: no cell, no chlorinator, lower initial spend. A salt system costs more to install, and the electrolytic cell is a wear part that typically needs replacing every three to seven years — a meaningful line item.

Day to day, however, salt pools usually win. Generating chlorine from a bag of salt is far cheaper than continually buying tablets or liquid chlorine, and in the Gulf's heat — which accelerates chlorine consumption — those savings compound. Over a ten-year horizon, a salt pool's lower consumable costs often offset its higher equipment outlay. Our **Aquora SaltStream** chlorinators are sized for GCC bather loads and temperatures to keep that balance favourable.

## The Coastal Corrosion Question

Here the Gulf demands respect. Salt accelerates corrosion, and many UAE pools sit within reach of coastal humidity and salt-laden air. Stainless fixtures, handrails, light housings, copper heating elements and nearby metalwork all face heightened risk in a salt environment.

This is manageable, not disqualifying. Specify marine-grade 316 stainless rather than 304, keep salinity within the chlorinator's recommended band, rinse fittings, and ensure proper bonding and earthing. Our **Aquora MarineGuard** fittings range is selected for exactly these conditions. For a rooftop or beachfront villa already battling airborne salt, this trade-off deserves a frank conversation before you commit.

## Maintenance Realities

Salt pools are lower-touch day to day — the chlorinator handles dosing automatically — but they are not maintenance-free. The cell needs periodic inspection and descaling, as Gulf water is often hard and scale builds quickly on the plates. Salinity, pH and stabiliser still need checking.

Chlorine pools demand more hands-on attention: regular testing and dosing, especially through peak summer when demand spikes. For absentee owners or holiday homes, the automation of a salt system is a genuine advantage.

## So Which Is Right for You?

For most Gulf villa owners swimming regularly, a salt system's comfort and lower running costs make a compelling case — provided corrosion is engineered out from the start. For coastal sites with heavy nearby metalwork, or budget-led projects, a well-managed chlorine pool remains a sound, proven choice.

Whichever route suits your property, our team can specify the right system, sizing and corrosion protection for your exact location. Talk to Aquora before your next build or upgrade.
```

Word count is roughly 620. Both house product lines (**Aquora SaltStream** chlorinators, **Aquora MarineGuard** fittings) are fictional and no real manufacturer brands are referenced. File available at `/Users/admin/Documents/aquora-saltwater-vs-chlorine.md` if you'd like it saved.

Here is the article.

```markdown
# Sizing a Pool Heat Pump for Year-Round Swimming in the UAE

A pool that only works for six months of the year is a pool working at half its value. In the UAE, the goal is straightforward: comfortable water from the peak of summer through the cooler December-to-February stretch, without an energy bill that makes you wince. Getting there starts with sizing the heat pump correctly. Undersize it and the water never quite reaches temperature on cold nights; oversize it and you have paid for capacity you rarely use. Here is how to get it right.

## Why an Inverter Heat Pump

A heat pump does not generate heat the way an element does. It moves heat from the surrounding air into the pool water, which is why it delivers far more energy than it consumes. The key measure is the Coefficient of Performance (COP): a unit running at a COP of 6 produces six kilowatts of heating for every kilowatt of electricity drawn. In Gulf conditions, where ambient air is warm for most of the year, heat pumps operate in their efficient range far more often than they would in a European climate.

Our Aquora ThermaCore inverter range takes this further. A fixed-speed unit runs flat out or not at all; an inverter compressor modulates its output, ramping down to hold a steady temperature once the pool is warm. That means quieter operation, less wear, and a meaningful drop in running cost over a full season. As a rule, inverter models hold their best COP figures across a wider band of conditions than fixed-speed equivalents.

## The Numbers That Drive Sizing

Three factors set the capacity you need:

- **Pool volume** — the body of water you are bringing up to temperature. A typical 8 × 4 m villa pool at 1.5 m average depth holds roughly 48,000 litres.
- **Surface area** — the larger and more important figure for ongoing heat loss. Over 90% of a pool's heat escapes from the surface through evaporation, so a wide, shallow pool loses more than its volume alone suggests.
- **Target temperature** — most swimmers are comfortable at 28–30°C. Each additional degree raises both the initial heat-up demand and the standing energy cost.

The greater the gap between your target temperature and the night-time air, the harder the unit works. This is why winter, not summer, dictates the size you buy.

## Sizing for UAE Winters

Summer needs almost no heating; the challenge is December to February, when night air can fall to 14–16°C and an unheated pool drifts to the low twenties. Size the unit to hold your target temperature on a cold winter night, and it will cope effortlessly the rest of the year.

For a standard uncovered villa pool of around 48,000 litres aiming for 29°C, a 13–17 kW inverter unit is typically the right window. Larger or fully exposed pools, or owners wanting reliable heat in January, should plan toward the upper end.

## The Single Biggest Efficiency Gain

No heat pump is efficient if the heat escapes overnight. A pool cover is the most effective upgrade you can make: by cutting evaporation it can reduce heat loss by 50–70%, letting a smaller unit do the same job and slashing running hours. Pair a correctly sized Aquora inverter with a quality cover and year-round swimming becomes genuinely economical.

## Talk to Aquora

Every pool is different. Share your dimensions, location and how you like your water, and our team will recommend the precise unit to keep you swimming all twelve months — efficiently.
```

# Designing a Modern Architectural Fountain: Nozzles, Pumps and Light

A well-designed fountain is engineering disguised as art. Whether it anchors a hotel forecourt in Dubai Marina or completes a private villa courtyard in Abu Dhabi, the difference between a feature that performs for a decade and one that disappoints within a season comes down to the discipline behind the water. At Aquora, we treat every architectural fountain as a hydraulic system first and a centrepiece second. Here is how the elements come together.

## Choosing the Right Nozzles

Nozzles define the visual language of the fountain, so specification should begin with the desired effect rather than the catalogue. A few workhorses cover most Gulf projects:

- **Smooth-bore jets** produce a clear, glassy stream ideal for formal reflecting pools and symmetrical layouts.
- **Aerating (foam) nozzles** entrain air to create a bright, white plume that reads strongly in daylight and resists wind distortion.
- **Cascade and fan nozzles** deliver broad, low-pressure sheets suited to terraces and water walls.
- **Ring and geyser arrays** drive the dramatic, height-led displays favoured for hospitality arrival sequences.

In our climate, wind is the silent enemy of crisp water shapes. The **Aquora Aeris** aerating range is engineered to hold form in gusty conditions, and we routinely recommend lowering jet heights to roughly twice the basin radius to keep spray within the catchment and off the surrounding stone.

## Pump Sizing and Hydraulics

Underspecified pumps are the most common cause of underwhelming fountains. Each nozzle has a defined flow and pressure demand, and the system must satisfy the sum of these at the point of delivery — not at the pump outlet. Friction losses through pipework, valves and fittings can quietly consume a third of available head, so generous pipe diameters and smooth bends are money well spent.

For larger schemes, the **Aquora HydroCore** variable-speed pump platform allows the display to be tuned after installation and dialled back during off-peak hours, trimming both energy use and noise. Always provide a dedicated, isolatable suction line and a properly sized balance tank; starving a pump in the GCC heat is a fast route to seal failure. Specify a strainer ahead of every pump and design for a turnover rate that keeps the water clear without overworking the plant.

## LED and Musical Control

Lighting is where an architectural fountain earns its evening presence. Submersible RGBW luminaires positioned beneath and behind jets make the water itself glow rather than simply illuminating the basin. The **Aquora Lumis** range uses low-voltage, IP68-rated fittings on DMX control, allowing scene programming that shifts through the night.

For signature hospitality installations, musical or "dancing" fountains synchronise pump speed, solenoid valves and lighting to an audio track via a central controller. The result is striking, but it demands robust electrical design, RCD protection and meticulous bonding throughout — non-negotiable around water.

## Maintenance for Gulf Conditions

High evaporation, hard water and fine desert dust make maintenance a design input, not an afterthought. Plan for auto-fill with a flow meter to flag leaks, and budget for regular descaling of nozzles and lenses where scaling dulls performance fastest. Wind-blown debris calls for accessible strainers and skimming. Treat the water — typically with bromine or a UV-assisted regime — to prevent algae in the heat, and ensure every pump, valve and light is serviceable without draining the entire basin.

Designed properly from the first sketch, an architectural fountain rewards the investment with years of effortless impact. Speak to the Aquora team to specify yours.
